executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned gates are green and live cron error count has cleared to zero this run, but whole-stack certification remains blocked by external marketing owner-loop residue (stale independent verification artifact, 5013 min old).
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Blocked-channel-recovery escalation is at critical level (956 repeats). Marketing independent verification remains fail-closed pending fresh outcome evidence.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks

findings.title: Blocked-channel-recovery escalation at 956 repeats
findings.mechanism: Health monitor reports blocked-channel-recovery:timeout has repeated 956 times with escalation_level=critical. Live cron no longer shows last_error, indicating the scheduler cleared the transient error state, but the underlying incident is unresolved.
findings.root_cause: blocked-channel-recovery job continues to timeout; root blockage persists despite cleared live-error flag.
findings.recommended_fix: Investigate why blocked-channel-recovery times out at 10m (last duration 326s); the escalation count of 956 demands owner intervention or architectural redesign of the recovery path.

findings.title: Marketing independent verification stale (5013 min old)
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification artifact is 5013 minutes old against a max-age threshold of 240 minutes, causing the health monitor to flag it as a stale runtime artifact.
findings.root_cause: Marketing owner loop has not produced fresh measurable outcome evidence or independent verification pass since last artifact refresh.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology is green with zero live errors this run
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection: 25 enabled/total jobs, 0 disabled, 2 running (system-health-monitor, agent-architecture-watchdog), 0 live last-error. This is improved from the previous run which showed 1 live last-error (blocked-channel-recovery).
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology is coherent; live error flag cleared since last watchdog run.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct cron inspection as source of truth. Live error clearing is positive but does not resolve the underlying blocked-channel-recovery incident.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Loop integrity, health-monitor blocker localization, and shared market-intelligence consumption remain coherent. Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining blockers are externalized correctly.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Resolve blocked-channel-recovery incident (956 repeat timeouts, escalation critical)
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: 956 consecutive timeouts is an emergency; scheduler cleared the live error flag but the underlying blockage persists.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either the recovery path is redesigned or the root blockage is cleared by an owner.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get a fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Artifact is 5013 min old (threshold 240 min); marketing still blocks whole-stack certification.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining adoption blocker or preserves truthful fail-closed outcome ownership.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the audit against the current live view: 25 enabled jobs, 0 disabled, 2 running, 0 live last-error. Live error count dropped from 1 to 0 since previous run (blocked-channel-recovery cleared from live error list).
Surfaced blocked-channel-recovery escalation at 956 repeats (critical) as the new highest-priority finding, even though live cron error flag cleared.
Updated architecture blocker map: no architecture-owned topology blockers. All remaining red localized to external owner loops (marketing stale artifact + blocked-channel-recovery incident).

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.
independent_verification.previous_artifact_summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Blocked-channel-recovery escalation at 956 repeats (critical)
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Even though the live cron error flag cleared this run, the health monitor escalation count of 956 for blocked-channel-recovery:timeout is a structural failure requiring immediate owner attention.

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Live error count dropped from 1 to 0 since previous run (blocked-channel-recovery cleared from live last_error), but underlying incident persists at 956 repeats (critical escalation).
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 25 total jobs, 25 enabled, 0 disabled, 2 running, and 0 live last-error jobs.
The two remaining blockers are external: blocked-channel-recovery incident (critical) and marketing outcome evidence (stale artifact).
blocked-channel-recovery escalation at 956 repeats demands architecture-level attention — this is no longer a transient issue.
